title: calamagrostis stricta subsp. groenlandica
intro: Calamagrostis stricta subsp. groenlandica, also known as Greenland bluegrass, is a subspecies of grass that is native to the Arctic tundra regions of North America and Greenland. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective against wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Greenland bluegrass include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Greenland bluegrass can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice for natural remedies and holistic health practices.
